title: When Should You Rekey Your Locks?—A Gainesville, FL Homeowners Guide
excerpt: Not sure when to rekey your locks? This Gainesville homeowners guide covers the key situations that call for a rekey, from lost keys to relationship changes and beyond.
author: Netta Kaiden
metaTitle: When to Rekey Locks: Gainesville Homeowners Guide | Be Secure Locksmith
metaDescription: Not sure when to rekey your locks? This Gainesville homeowners guide covers the key situations that call for a rekey, from lost keys to relationship changes and beyond.
body: Most Gainesville homeowners don’t give their locks a second thought until something goes wrong. But rekeying is a smart, proactive step that costs less than a full lock replacement and takes far less time. Instead of swapping out hardware, rekeying changes the internal pins inside your existing lock so old keys stop working. If a specific situation has you questioning your home’s access control, here’s what you need to know.
When Should You Rekey Locks After Losing a Key?

A missing key doesn’t always mean trouble, but it does mean you can no longer account for who has access to your home. Rather than waiting to see if it turns up, a rekey removes the risk entirely. Your existing locks stay in place and keep working normally, but the lost key becomes useless.
This also applies when a key was handed out and never returned. Common examples include:

• A former pet sitter or dog walker
  • A contractor or repair technician
  • A neighbor who held a spare key temporarily
  • A family member who no longer needs regular access
Gainesville homeowners who use professional lock rekeying services can have this completed on-site in a single visit without swapping any hardware.
Does a Change in Household Members Mean You Should Rekey?

Yes. Any time someone who previously had access is no longer part of the household, rekeying is worth doing. This includes a roommate moving out, a relationship ending, or a live-in caregiver finishing their time in your home. Rekeying is less about trust and more about maintaining clear boundaries for your own security. Once someone has a physical copy of your key, there’s no way to know whether duplicates were made. A rekey closes that loop cleanly.
If you manage rental property, this situation comes up between every tenant. Our property management locksmith services are designed for landlords who need fast, reliable rekeying on a regular basis.
Should You Rekey After a Break-In Attempt or Security Concern?

If your home has been broken into or someone attempted to access it without permission, rekeying is typically part of the response. Even when the locks were not bypassed, a break-in attempt can compromise your key-based security in ways that aren’t immediately visible.

When a door or frame was damaged, we can also assist with lock repair before completing the rekey. Handling both in one visit means your entry points are fully restored rather than just patched. If you’ve noticed signs of unwanted activity on your property, or a nearby neighbor has experienced a break-in, rekeying is a low-cost way to update your access control without replacing hardware that’s still functioning well.
Is Rekeying a Good Idea When You Upgrade to Smart Locks?

If you’re adding a smart lock to your front door while keeping traditional keyed locks on side or back doors, coordinating access across those entry points matters. Rekeying the remaining keyed locks to a single key keeps things simple and reduces the number of keys you need to manage.

We handle smart lock installation alongside rekeying, so both services can be completed in the same visit. That gives you a cleaner, more organized access system without scheduling multiple appointments.
How Often Should Gainesville Homeowners Rekey as a Routine Step?

There’s no fixed schedule that fits every household, but reviewing your lock access once a year is a reasonable baseline. For homeowners who have lived in the same house for several years, it’s easy to lose track of how many copies of your key are circulating. A routine rekey is a low-cost way to reset that access and start fresh. It costs less than most homeowners expect and requires no new hardware.
Signs it’s time for a routine rekey include:

• You’ve lived in your home for several years with no access review
  • Multiple people have been given keys over time and access was never revoked
  • You recently completed a home renovation with outside contractors on-site
  • You’re simply unsure how many copies of your key exist
If you want to understand your full range of options before scheduling anything, our residential locksmith services page covers everything available for homeowners.
